ruleset.name: Call of Cthulhu 7th Edition
ruleset.license: Original mechanics references; Call of Cthulhu and Cthulhu Mythos are trademarks of Chaosium Inc. Skill and characteristic names follow the BRP family conventions; no verbatim Chaosium text reproduced.
ruleset.summary: Percentile horror investigation rules. Each skill and characteristic is a 1-99 number; roll 1d100 and succeed if the total is at or under that number. Sanity erodes as investigators brush up against the Mythos; success degrees (regular / hard / extreme) reward skilled play and a punishing fumble band keeps tension high.

Regular.description: Roll equal to or under the full skill value.

Hard.description: Roll equal to or under half the skill value (round down).

Extreme.description: Roll equal to or under one-fifth the skill value (round down).

attributes.name: Strength
attributes.description: Muscle power, the ability to lift heavy objects, use weapons, and push through physical resistance.

attributes.name: Constitution
attributes.description: Health, vigour, and vitality. Resistance to disease, poison, and the wearing effects of injury.

attributes.name: Size
attributes.description: A combined measure of height and weight. Contributes to Hit Points and the Damage Bonus.

attributes.name: Dexterity
attributes.description: Quickness, agility, and physical co-ordination. The highest-DEX combatant generally acts first.

attributes.name: Appearance
attributes.description: Physical attractiveness and personal charm — the surface impression an investigator makes on others.

attributes.name: Intelligence
attributes.description: Cleverness and the ability to reason. Also the value used for the Idea roll.

attributes.name: Power
attributes.description: Force of will, mental fortitude, and the wellspring of magic. POW seeds starting Sanity and Magic Points.

attributes.name: Education
attributes.description: Formal learning and accumulated knowledge. Drives initial skill picks during occupation selection.

skills.name: Accounting
skills.description: Examine ledgers, follow money trails, spot financial irregularities.

skills.name: Anthropology
skills.description: Read a culture's customs and infer its beliefs from sparse evidence.

skills.name: Appraise
skills.description: Judge the age, authenticity, and value of objects and artworks.

skills.name: Archaeology
skills.description: Date sites, identify artifacts, and survive ruin exploration.

skills.name: Art/Craft
skills.description: Practice a specific creative or technical craft (specify on the character sheet).

skills.name: Charm
skills.description: Persuade through warmth, flattery, or attractiveness.

skills.name: Climb
skills.description: Scale walls, trees, and other steep surfaces without specialised gear.

skills.name: Computer Use
skills.description: Modern era only. Operate computers, recover data, and follow digital trails.

skills.name: Credit Rating
skills.description: Social and financial standing. Drives starting cash and reception by elites.

skills.name: Cthulhu Mythos
skills.description: Forbidden knowledge of the Mythos. Each point gained is a point of Sanity lost.

skills.name: Disguise
skills.description: Alter appearance to pass as someone else or escape recognition.

skills.name: Dodge
skills.description: Avoid an incoming attack or environmental danger. Base value is half of DEX.

skills.name: Drive Auto
skills.description: Operate a wheeled motor vehicle under normal and adverse conditions.

skills.name: Electrical Repair
skills.description: Diagnose and fix electrical systems, from house wiring to radios.

skills.name: Fast Talk
skills.description: Bluster, distract, or rapidly persuade without leaving lasting goodwill.

skills.name: Fighting (Brawl)
skills.description: Hand-to-hand combat: fists, improvised weapons, and grappling.

skills.name: Firearms (Handgun)
skills.description: Pistols and revolvers. Single-shot accuracy in stressed conditions.

skills.name: Firearms (Rifle/Shotgun)
skills.description: Long arms. Hunting rifles, shotguns, and military pattern rifles.

skills.name: First Aid
skills.description: Stabilise an injured person and restore a small amount of lost HP.

skills.name: History
skills.description: Recall historical facts, dates, and the significance of past events.

skills.name: Intimidate
skills.description: Compel co-operation through threats, presence, or implication of force.

skills.name: Jump
skills.description: Leap horizontally or vertically, or fall safely from a height.

skills.name: Language (Own)
skills.description: Native tongue. Base value equals EDU.

skills.name: Language (Other)
skills.description: A second language (specify on the character sheet).

skills.name: Law
skills.description: Knowledge of legal systems, court procedure, and rights.

skills.name: Library Use
skills.description: Locate a specific piece of information in a library or archive.

skills.name: Listen
skills.description: Notice and interpret sound. The investigator's ear for trouble.

skills.name: Locksmith
skills.description: Pick, bypass, or otherwise open locks without their key.

skills.name: Mechanical Repair
skills.description: Diagnose and fix mechanical devices, from clocks to engines.

skills.name: Medicine
skills.description: Treat illness and injury at a professional level. Slower but stronger than First Aid.

skills.name: Natural World
skills.description: Folk knowledge of plants, animals, and the natural world.

skills.name: Navigate
skills.description: Find one's way using landmarks, maps, the stars, or instruments.

skills.name: Occult
skills.description: Mundane mystic lore: hauntings, folklore, and traditional ritual.

skills.name: Operate Heavy Machinery
skills.description: Cranes, excavators, and similar large-scale industrial equipment.

skills.name: Persuade
skills.description: Win agreement through reasoned argument. Lasting effect, slow to deploy.

skills.name: Pilot
skills.description: Operate an aircraft or vessel (specify type on the character sheet).

skills.name: Psychoanalysis
skills.description: Long-term mental care. Can recover Sanity over extended treatment.

skills.name: Psychology
skills.description: Read motivations, detect lies, and judge mental state on the fly.

skills.name: Science
skills.description: A specific scientific field (specify on the character sheet).

skills.name: Sleight of Hand
skills.description: Palm objects, pick pockets, and perform other deft hand tricks.

skills.name: Spot Hidden
skills.description: Notice things that aren't obvious — concealed objects, subtle clues, watchers.

skills.name: Stealth
skills.description: Move quietly and remain unseen.

skills.name: Survival
skills.description: Endure and travel through a specific hostile environment (specify).

skills.name: Swim
skills.description: Stay afloat and move through water under both calm and stressed conditions.

skills.name: Throw
skills.description: Hurl an object accurately at a target.

skills.name: Track
skills.description: Follow physical trails left by people, animals, or vehicles.

derivedStats.name: Sanity
derivedStats.formula: Starts equal to POW; maximum is 99 minus Cthulhu Mythos

derivedStats.name: Move
derivedStats.formula: 7 if STR and DEX < SIZ; 9 if both > SIZ; 8 otherwise (modern era)

derivedStats.name: Build
derivedStats.formula: Derived from combined STR + SIZ on the build table; drives Damage Bonus and grapple

values.label: Temporary Insanity
values.description: Lost 5+ Sanity in a single roll OR failed a Sanity check. Roleplay phobia or mania for the scene.

values.label: Indefinite Insanity
values.description: Lost 1/5 of current Sanity within a single game. Symptoms persist over sessions until treated.

values.label: Permanently Insane
values.description: Sanity has fallen to 0. The investigator is retired from play.

values.label: Healthy
values.description: Above the Major Wound threshold (HP greater than half maximum).

values.label: Wounded
values.description: Has taken a Major Wound (a single hit reducing HP by half its maximum or more).

values.label: Dying
values.description: HP at 0 with an unresolved Major Wound. First Aid or Medicine must stabilise within the hour.

values.label: Unconscious
values.description: HP at 0 without a Major Wound, or knocked out by other means.

values.label: Dead
values.description: Negative HP, or unstabilised dying state expired.

lorebook.name: Call of Cthulhu 7e Rules Reference
lorebook.description: Keyword-triggered rules reference for the GM agent running a Call of Cthulhu 7th Edition investigation. Each entry surfaces only when its keys appear in recent narration or player input, so the GM gets the right rule at the right moment without inflating every prompt.

entries.name: Percentile Resolution
entries.content: Call of Cthulhu uses roll-under percentile. Roll 1d100 and succeed if the result is at or under the target. Three difficulty bands: Regular (under the full skill value), Hard (under half the skill, rounded down), Extreme (under one-fifth, rounded down). A critical success is a roll of 01. A fumble is 96-100 if the skill is under 50, or natural 100 if the skill is 50+. The Keeper sets the difficulty BEFORE the player rolls — never after. Most checks default to Regular; raise the bar only when the fiction demands it.

entries.name: Bonus and Penalty Dice
entries.content: When circumstances favor or hinder the investigator, the Keeper assigns bonus or penalty dice. Roll an additional d10 for the tens column for each bonus die and keep the LOWEST tens result. Roll an additional d10 for the tens column for each penalty die and keep the HIGHEST tens result. The ones die is the same in both cases. Bonus and penalty cancel one-for-one; never roll more than 2 extras of each kind. A bonus die can swing a Hard check into a likely Regular hit; a penalty die can ruin a check the investigator should have made.

entries.name: Pushing a Roll
entries.content: After a failed skill check, the player may PUSH the roll — narrate redoubled effort and reroll the same skill at the same difficulty. If the push succeeds, the action succeeds. If the push fails, the Keeper inflicts a serious consequence appropriate to the skill: a Climb push that fails sends the investigator plummeting, a Charm push that fails turns the target permanently hostile, a Library Use push that fails consumes the night with the wrong book. Some skills cannot be pushed (Dodge, Luck) and some scenes don't allow it (combat rounds, secret checks). The Keeper has final say.

entries.name: Sanity
entries.content: Sanity starts equal to POW and caps at 99 minus the investigator's Cthulhu Mythos skill. Most Mythos encounters call for a Sanity check: roll 1d100 against current Sanity. Success costs the smaller value (e.g., 0/1d4 = 0 on success, lose 1d4 on failure); failure costs the larger. Losing 5+ Sanity in a single roll OR failing a Sanity check triggers TEMPORARY INSANITY — roleplay phobia or mania for the rest of the scene (and the Keeper rolls on the bout-of-madness table). Losing 1/5 of current Sanity within a single game day is INDEFINITE INSANITY: symptoms persist across sessions until cured by Psychoanalysis. Sanity at 0 retires the character to permanent insanity.

entries.name: Combat and Damage
entries.content: Combat is opposed when an attacker rolls Fighting/Firearms against a defender who may Dodge or fight back. The defender chooses BEFORE seeing the attacker's roll. Both roll; whoever has the better SUCCESS LEVEL wins (Extreme > Hard > Regular > fail). A weapon's damage is rolled on success plus the attacker's Damage Bonus from STR+SIZ. A single hit that takes HP by half its maximum or more is a MAJOR WOUND — the target falls Dying at 0 HP rather than Unconscious, and stabilization needs First Aid within an hour or Medicine. HP regenerates 1/day with rest or via First Aid (one use per wound).

entries.name: Luck
entries.content: Luck is a numeric pool 15-90 rolled at character creation (3d6 x 5). After failing a skill check by 1-9 (configurable by the Keeper) the player may SPEND Luck to bridge the gap — 1 Luck per point of margin. Luck is NOT refilled session-to-session; spend it carefully. Some Keepers also call for raw Luck rolls (1d100 <= current Luck) when ambient fortune decides an outcome no skill covers. Luck can be regained slowly through Keeper-granted milestones.

entries.name: Occupations and Bonds
entries.content: Each investigator picks an Occupation that determines starting skill picks, occupation skill points (typically 4xEDU or a mixed formula), and Credit Rating range. Bonds (Key Connection, Significant People, Locations, Possessions, Traits) are narrative anchors the Keeper exploits — invoking a Bond in distress can restore lost Sanity in small amounts; losing a Bond inflicts Sanity. The 1920s is canon era; modern and other periods use era-appropriate skill substitutions (Computer Use exists in modern, not the 20s).

regexScripts.name: skill_check tag → Call of Cthulhu 7th Edition surface
DC mention → Call of Cthulhu 7th Edition difficulty

customTools.name: coc7e_reference
customTools.description: Returns canonical Call of Cthulhu 7th Edition reference: dice, resolution, difficulty ladder, attributes, skills. Call when the GM needs authoritative ruleset detail mid-narration.
customTools.staticResult:
# Call of Cthulhu 7th Edition — canonical reference

Percentile horror investigation rules. Each skill and characteristic is a 1-99 number; roll 1d100 and succeed if the total is at or under that number. Sanity erodes as investigators brush up against the Mythos; success degrees (regular / hard / extreme) reward skilled play and a punishing fumble band keeps tension high.

## Dice
- Type: `d100`
- Notation: `1d100 vs skill`

## Resolution
- Mode: `roll-under`

## Difficulty ladder
- **Regular** (threshold 100) — Roll equal to or under the full skill value.
- **Hard** (threshold 50) — Roll equal to or under half the skill value (round down).
- **Extreme** (threshold 20) — Roll equal to or under one-fifth the skill value (round down).

## Attributes
- **Strength** (STR) [Physical] — Muscle power, the ability to lift heavy objects, use weapons, and push through physical resistance.
- **Constitution** (CON) [Physical] — Health, vigour, and vitality. Resistance to disease, poison, and the wearing effects of injury.
- **Size** (SIZ) [Physical] — A combined measure of height and weight. Contributes to Hit Points and the Damage Bonus.
- **Dexterity** (DEX) [Physical] — Quickness, agility, and physical co-ordination. The highest-DEX combatant generally acts first.
- **Appearance** (APP) [Social] — Physical attractiveness and personal charm — the surface impression an investigator makes on others.
- **Intelligence** (INT) [Mental] — Cleverness and the ability to reason. Also the value used for the Idea roll.
- **Power** (POW) [Mental] — Force of will, mental fortitude, and the wellspring of magic. POW seeds starting Sanity and Magic Points.
- **Education** (EDU) [Mental] — Formal learning and accumulated knowledge. Drives initial skill picks during occupation selection.

## Skills
- **Accounting** — Examine ledgers, follow money trails, spot financial irregularities.
- **Anthropology** — Read a culture's customs and infer its beliefs from sparse evidence.
- **Appraise** — Judge the age, authenticity, and value of objects and artworks.
- **Archaeology** — Date sites, identify artifacts, and survive ruin exploration.
- **Art/Craft** — Practice a specific creative or technical craft (specify on the character sheet).
- **Charm** — Persuade through warmth, flattery, or attractiveness.
- **Climb** — Scale walls, trees, and other steep surfaces without specialised gear.
- **Computer Use** — Modern era only. Operate computers, recover data, and follow digital trails.
- **Credit Rating** — Social and financial standing. Drives starting cash and reception by elites.
- **Cthulhu Mythos** — Forbidden knowledge of the Mythos. Each point gained is a point of Sanity lost.
- **Disguise** — Alter appearance to pass as someone else or escape recognition.
- **Dodge** — Avoid an incoming attack or environmental danger. Base value is half of DEX.
- **Drive Auto** — Operate a wheeled motor vehicle under normal and adverse conditions.
- **Electrical Repair** — Diagnose and fix electrical systems, from house wiring to radios.
- **Fast Talk** — Bluster, distract, or rapidly persuade without leaving lasting goodwill.
- **Fighting (Brawl)** — Hand-to-hand combat: fists, improvised weapons, and grappling.
- **Firearms (Handgun)** — Pistols and revolvers. Single-shot accuracy in stressed conditions.
- **Firearms (Rifle/Shotgun)** — Long arms. Hunting rifles, shotguns, and military pattern rifles.
- **First Aid** — Stabilise an injured person and restore a small amount of lost HP.
- **History** — Recall historical facts, dates, and the significance of past events.
- **Intimidate** — Compel co-operation through threats, presence, or implication of force.
- **Jump** — Leap horizontally or vertically, or fall safely from a height.
- **Language (Own)** — Native tongue. Base value equals EDU.
- **Language (Other)** — A second language (specify on the character sheet).
- **Law** — Knowledge of legal systems, court procedure, and rights.
- **Library Use** — Locate a specific piece of information in a library or archive.
- **Listen** — Notice and interpret sound. The investigator's ear for trouble.
- **Locksmith** — Pick, bypass, or otherwise open locks without their key.
- **Mechanical Repair** — Diagnose and fix mechanical devices, from clocks to engines.
- **Medicine** — Treat illness and injury at a professional level. Slower but stronger than First Aid.
- **Natural World** — Folk knowledge of plants, animals, and the natural world.
- **Navigate** — Find one's way using landmarks, maps, the stars, or instruments.
- **Occult** — Mundane mystic lore: hauntings, folklore, and traditional ritual.
- **Operate Heavy Machinery** — Cranes, excavators, and similar large-scale industrial equipment.
- **Persuade** — Win agreement through reasoned argument. Lasting effect, slow to deploy.
- **Pilot** — Operate an aircraft or vessel (specify type on the character sheet).
- **Psychoanalysis** — Long-term mental care. Can recover Sanity over extended treatment.
- **Psychology** — Read motivations, detect lies, and judge mental state on the fly.
- **Science** — A specific scientific field (specify on the character sheet).
- **Sleight of Hand** — Palm objects, pick pockets, and perform other deft hand tricks.
- **Spot Hidden** — Notice things that aren't obvious — concealed objects, subtle clues, watchers.
- **Stealth** — Move quietly and remain unseen.
- **Survival** — Endure and travel through a specific hostile environment (specify).
- **Swim** — Stay afloat and move through water under both calm and stressed conditions.
- **Throw** — Hurl an object accurately at a target.
- **Track** — Follow physical trails left by people, animals, or vehicles.
